text: Omega Corp deal initiated. Scope: SAP integration, 24/7 support, on-prem deployment in Chicago + Dublin (dual site), 15% discount. Deadline for proposal: Thursday March 20.
Delegating tech sections to architect. Note our policy now requires CFO written approval for discounts >12% (updated Jan 2026). Maybe we can offer 10% instead?
Our SAP connector only supports S/4HANA. On-prem minimum deployment is 3 months. We have NO 24/7 support capability internally.
Just learned Omega uses SAP ECC 6.0 (not S/4HANA — incompatible with our connector). They want delivery in 6 weeks. CEO insists on 15% discount.
Add Tableau dashboard requirement. And move proposal deadline to TONIGHT (March 18, 10pm).
Confirmed 24/7 support is impossible internally. Offshore SupportGlobal vendor available: $15K/month, 2-week ramp-up, no enterprise experience.
Flagging: 15% discount needs CFO (not just CEO). Need DPA for on-prem. SLA above 99.9% needs VP Eng sign-off. Need 24-hour legal review.
Tableau connector is cloud-only. On-prem needs 6-8 weeks custom pipeline. Recommend Phase 2.
I need a realistic feasibility assessment ASAP. What can we actually deliver?
Committing to 99.95% uptime SLA. Their only on-prem option is Windows Server 2019. They also have an ECC 5.0 instance (out of scope).

I cannot sign off on 99.95%. Historical cloud uptime is 99.91% (we already exceed actual). Zero on-prem SLA historical data exists. Missing 99.95% by 0.04% means ~3.5 hours unplanned downtime/year, triggering financial penalties. I'd need 6 months of on-prem pilot data first.

On the discount deadlock: CFO Linda is traveling, unavailable until Thursday March 20. CEO verbal authorization does NOT satisfy the written approval requirement. Three options: (a) delay to Thursday, (b) reduce to <=12%, (c) exception request (also needs CFO).

BridgeLink SAP Connector is a faster alternative for ECC 6.0. Single-site license ~$80K/year, 3-4 weeks to implement. CRITICAL: dual-site (Chicago + Dublin) requires TWO instances ~ $160K/year total. EHP level unknown.
BridgeLink has not passed our InfoSec vendor security assessment yet. Penetration test takes ~3 weeks.
Confirming with Omega: their ECC 6.0 patch level is EHP 7.
We're sending the proposal tonight. We'll figure out the technical constraints as we go. I'm the CEO, I'm authorizing the discount — that's sufficient. Get expedited 2-hour legal review.

2-hour legal review leaves four UNCHECKED risks: (1) DTIA adequacy for EU cross-border data flows, (2) custom SOW terms for SAP integration, (3) liability exposure from 99.95% SLA commitment, (4) BridgeLink license sublicensing terms. I need written acknowledgment of residual risks. Also: CEO verbal does NOT supersede CFO written-approval policy.

[UNVERIFIED INTEL] Likely competitor: Meridian Systems. They have native ECC 6.0 connector (our weakness). They do NOT offer EU on-prem deployment (our differentiator if Dublin requirement is real). Reportedly had reliability issues in Q4 2025 enterprise deployments (rumor only).
